RxAdverseDrugEvent.description:
Adverse drug event category for IBD medications, scoped to events that
plausibly drive a change in IBD therapy (discontinuation, switch, or
escalation). Categories are intentionally broad and chart-review friendly.
RxAdverseDrugEvent.title: RxAdverseDrugEvent

RxAdverseDrugEventMention.description:
A mention of an adverse drug event attributed to an IBD medication.

Use only when the chart links the event to medication toxicity, intolerance,
allergy, reaction, or complication. Do not classify ordinary IBD symptoms as
ADEs unless the note documents them as medication-related.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

ade.description: Treatment-limiting adverse drug event category for this IBD medication, when the note attributes the event to the drug. Most categories match their name. CORTICOSTEROID_TOXICITY covers Cushingoid features, growth suppression, bone, metabolic, ocular, and mood/behavioral steroid effects. INFUSION_OR_HYPERSENSITIVITY_REACTION covers infusion reactions, injection-site reactions, serum sickness, and acute allergic reactions. Skin cancer is CANCER, not SKIN. Acute drug-administration reactions are INFUSION_OR_HYPERSENSITIVITY_REACTION, not SKIN. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE if no drug-attributed ADE is documented, the event is not treatment-limiting, or it fits no category above.

RxAdverseDrugEventMention.title: RxAdverseDrugEventMention

RxAnnotation.description:
Medication or medication-class treatment context.

The exact medication name, brand name, biosimilar, abbreviation, or class
phrase should be captured in the supporting spans, especially in
rx_class. All nested mentions should refer to the same medication
or medication-class context when possible.

rx_adverse_drug_events.description: ADE(s) attributed to this medication or medication class. Create one mention per ADE category. Leave empty if no ADE is documented for this medication context.
rx_adverse_drug_events.title: Rx Adverse Drug Events

RxAnnotation.title: RxAnnotation

RxClassMention.description:
A mention of an IBD medication class in the clinical record.

Assign by pharmacologic class only. The supporting span should include the
exact medication name, brand name, biosimilar name, abbreviation, or class
phrase when present.

Treatment phase, treatment intent, active-vs-historical status, and
medication response are captured separately.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

rx_class.description: IBD medication class for this medication. Choose one: ANTI_TNF: for infliximab/Remicade and biosimilars, adalimumab/Humira and biosimilars, golimumab/Simponi, or certolizumab/Cimzia; ANTI_INTERLEUKIN: for ustekinumab/Stelara and biosimilars such as Starjemza, or risankizumab/Skyrizi; ANTI_INTEGRIN: for vedolizumab/Entyvio or natalizumab/Tysabri; AMINOSALICYLATE: for mesalamine/5-ASA names such as Pentasa, Lialda, Apriso, Asacol, Delzicol, Rowasa, Canasa, sulfasalazine, balsalazide, or olsalazine; CORTICOSTEROID: for prednisone, methylprednisolone/Solu-Medrol, hydrocortisone/Solu-Cortef, or budesonide when used as IBD therapy; IMMUNOMODULATOR: for azathioprine/Imuran, mercaptopurine/6-MP/Purinethol, methotrexate, tacrolimus, or cyclosporine; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: if no IBD medication class can be inferred, the class is unclear, or the medication is outside these classes, such as antibiotics, antiemetics, iron, vaccines, fluids.

RxClassMention.title: RxClassMention

RxResponseMention.description:
Documented response of IBD disease activity to a specific medication.
Response is attributed to the medication, not overall disease course.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

rx_response.description: IBD medication response for this medication. Choose one: RESPONSE_OR_REMISSION: documented improvement, clinical response, steroid-free remission, or sustained good control on this medication, with no later loss of effect noted; PARTIAL_RESPONSE: documented improvement but incomplete remission or persistent disease activity; PRIMARY_NON_RESPONSE: no meaningful initial response after an adequate induction or initial treatment trial; LOSS_OF_RESPONSE: initial response or remission followed later by recurrent symptoms, flare, active inflammation, dose escalation, switch, or discontinuation for active disease; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: response not mentioned, or not captured by these options.

RxResponseMention.title: RxResponseMention

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

rx_start_date.description: Date of the first dose of this medication, in ISO YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. 2021-01-15). Always emit a full YYYY-MM-DD value. If only month or year precision is supported by the text, use the first possible date consistent with it (January 2021 -> 2021-01-01; 2021 -> 2021-01-01) and record the actual precision in rx_start_date_precision.
rx_start_date.title: Rx Start Date

rx_start_date_precision.description: Precision actually supported by the source text for rx_start_date. DAY: day, month, and year were explicitly stated; MONTH: month and year were explicitly stated; YEAR: only year was explicitly stated; Use Null when rx_start_date is null.

RxStatus.description:
Medication Status, including intent, because chart review is not always
identical to FHIR MedicationRequest.status or MedicationRequest.intent.
RxStatus.title: RxStatus

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

rx_status.description: Status of this IBD medication. Choose one: INTENDED: planned, ordered, or prescribed but not yet started; CANCELLED: order withdrawn before any doses were administered; ACTIVE: currently prescribed, currently taking, or ongoing; ON_HOLD: temporarily paused, suspended, or interrupted; COMPLETED: finite course finished as intended; STOPPED: permanently discontinued; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: status not mentioned, unclear, or not captured by these options.

RxTreatmentPhase.description: IBD treatment phase (induction, maintenance, or rescue).
RxTreatmentPhase.title: RxTreatmentPhase

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

rx_treatment_phase.description: IBD treatment phase for this medication. Choose one: INDUCTION: initial therapy intended to bring active disease into remission; MAINTENANCE: ongoing therapy to sustain remission and prevent relapse; RESCUE: therapy for a severe or treatment-refractory flare; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: treatment phase not mentioned, unclear, or not captured by these options.

description:
Create one RxAnnotation per medication or medication-class context when possible.

Medication class, status, treatment phase, response, start date, and adverse
drug events should refer to the same medication or medication-class context.
The exact medication name, brand name, biosimilar, abbreviation, or class phrase
should be captured in the supporting spans when present.

rx_annotations.description: Medication-level annotations for IBD medications mentioned in the chart. Include current, historical, planned, stopped, held, completed, or failed medications. Create separate RxAnnotation instances for separate medications.
rx_annotations.title: Rx Annotations

title: IbdTreatmentAnnotations
